items.title: USDA posts SNAP food restriction waiver tracker with state start dates
items.summary: USDA is approving state waivers to restrict SNAP purchases of items like soda and candy, and published a tracker listing target implementation dates. Examples on the tracker include Florida (04/20/26) restricting soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts and Montana (09/30/26) restricting high-sugar beverages, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts.

items.title: USDA finalizes SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance required by Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s final rule updates SNAP retailer stocking standards, including reclassifying certain items (like butter, jerky, and snack bars) so they no longer count as staple foods for retailer authorization (though they remain eligible for purchase). Starting Nov. 4, 2026, most SNAP retailers (other than specialty stores) must stock at least 7 varieties in each of the 4 staple categories (dairy, fruits/vegetables, grains, and protein) and include a perishable variety in at least 3 of the 4 categories.

items.title: USDA Summer EBT (SUN Bucks) provides $120 per eligible child for summer groceries
items.summary: USDA says Summer EBT provides $120 in grocery benefits per eligible school-age child when school is out for summer. Many eligible children will be enrolled automatically if their household already receives SNAP, TANF, or similar benefits, but some families will need to apply through their state, Tribe, or territory.

items.title: CMS proposes Medicaid payment caps for some supplemental payments (state directed payments)
items.summary: CMS published a proposed rule that would cap certain Medicaid managed care state directed payments (SDPs) at 100% of the total published Medicare payment rate in Medicaid expansion states and 110% in non-expansion states (or 100% of the state plan rate if no Medicare rate exists). CMS also proposes extending the payment-rate limit to all SDPs for rating periods beginning on or after Jan. 1, 2029, and would phase down grandfathered SDPs by 10 percentage points annually starting with the first rating period on or after Jan. 1, 2028.

items.title: Direct Express transitions to Fifth Third Bank as new financial agent
items.summary: SSA says Treasury’s Bureau of the Fiscal Service selected Fifth Third Bank as the new financial agent for the Direct Express program. New Direct Express enrollments with Fifth Third Bank begin in May 2026, and existing cardholders will begin transitioning later in 2026 or early 2027 with advance notice.

items.title: HUD sets HOTMA (Sections 102 and 104) enforcement date for most PHAs: Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-15 sets an enforcement date of Jan. 1, 2027 for compliance with HOTMA Sections 102 and 104 for all PHAs except MTW PHAs and PHAs exclusively using HUD’s Family Reporting Software (FRS). HUD notes that PHAs that start annual reexams 120 days before the effective date may need to begin collecting and verifying family income under HOTMA policies starting in September 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: USDA expands whole and 2% milk options in child nutrition programs (effective June 8, 2026)
items.summary: USDA issued a final rule allowing schools and child and adult care providers in child nutrition programs to offer whole milk and reduced-fat (2%) milk to participants age 2 and older. The rule is effective June 8, 2026, and USDA will accept comments on the final rule through June 8, 2026.

items.title: Oklahoma LIHEAP crisis assistance (ECAP) application cutoff: May 22, 2026 (midnight)
items.summary: Oklahoma Human Services says it will stop accepting Energy Crisis Assistance Program (ECAP) applications at midnight Friday, May 22, 2026, and online applications can be submitted until 12:00 A.M. CST Saturday, May 23, 2026. The agency also lists anticipated FFY 2026 open-enrollment dates: Winter Heating (Jan. 6, 2026) and Summer Cooling (July 14, 2026).
